items.name: Get status

Getting data from a machine-readable version of the Apache status page.
For more information see Apache Module [mod_status](https://httpd.apache.org/docs/current/mod/mod_status.html).

items.description: The number of workers in closing state.

items.name: Workers finishing
items.description: The number of workers in finishing state.

items.name: Requests per second
items.description:
It is calculated as a rate of change for the "Total requests" statistics.
`ReqPerSec` is not used, as it counts the average since the last Apache server start.

items.name: Bytes per second
items.description:
It is calculated as a rate of change for total bytes statistics.
`BytesPerSec` is not used, as it counts the average since the last Apache server start.

items.description: The total bytes served.

items.name: Workers waiting for connection

items.name: Overall system health status
items.description:
MIB: SW-MIB
The current operational status of the switch.The states are as follow:
online(1) means the switch is accessible by an external Fibre Channel port
offline(2) means the switch is not accessible
testing(3) means the switch is in a built-in test mode and is not accessible by an external

items.name: CPU utilization

MIB: SW-MIB
System's CPU usage.

items.name: Memory utilization
items.description:
MIB: SW-MIB
Memory utilization in %.

ICMP loss
items.description: The percentage of lost packets.

items.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

items.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

The current value (reading) of the sensor.

The fan value will be in RPM(revolution per minute)

item_prototypes.description: MIB: SW-MIB

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of th

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCoun

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of t

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them fro

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n' then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000' to`n+499,999'.  For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accu

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

items.name: Number of non-deleted documents
items.description:
The total number of non-deleted documents across all primary shards assigned to the selected nodes.
This number is based on the documents in Lucene segments and may include the documents from nested fields.

items.name: Cluster uptime
items.description: Uptime duration in seconds since JVM has last started.

items.name: Total size of all file stores
items.description: The total size in bytes of all file stores across all selected nodes.

items.name: Total available size to JVM in all file stores
items.description:
The total number of bytes available to JVM in the file stores across all selected nodes.
Depending on OS or process-level restrictions, this number may be less than nodes.fs.free_in_byes.
This is the actual amount of free disk space the selected Elasticsearch nodes can use.

items.description: The number of selected nodes with the ingest role.

items.description: The number of shards whose allocation has been delayed by the timeout settings.

items.name: Indices with shards assigned to nodes
items.description: The total number of indices with shards assigned to the selected nodes.

items.name: Task max waiting in queue
items.description: The time expressed in seconds since the earliest initiated task is waiting for being performed.

items.name: Cluster health status
items.description:
Health status of the cluster, based on the state of its primary and replica shards. Statuses are:
green
All shards are assigned.
yellow

items.name: ClassLoading: Loaded class count
items.description: Displays number of classes that are currently loaded in the Java virtual machine.

items.name: OperatingSystem: File descriptors maximum count
items.description: This is the number of file descriptors we can have opened in the same process, as determined by the operating system. You can never have more file descriptors than this number.

items.name: OperatingSystem: File descriptors opened
items.description: This is the number of opened file descriptors at the moment, if this reaches the MaxFileDescriptorCount, the application will throw an IOException: Too many open files. This could mean you are opening file descriptors and never closing them.

items.name: OperatingSystem: Process CPU Load
items.description: ProcessCpuLoad represents the CPU load in this process.

items.name: Threading: Daemon thread count
items.description: Number of daemon threads running.

items.name: Threading: Peak thread count
items.description: Maximum number of threads being executed at the same time since the JVM was started or the peak was reset.

items.name: Threading: Thread count
items.description: The number of threads running at the current moment.

items.name: ClassLoading: Total loaded class count
items.description: Displays the total number of classes that have been loaded since the Java virtual machine has started execution.

items.name: ClassLoading: Unloaded class count
items.description: Displays the total number of classes that have been loaded since the Java virtual machine has started execution.

items.name: Compilation: Name of the current JIT compiler
items.description: Displays the total number of classes unloaded since the Java virtual machine has started execution.

items.name: Compilation: Accumulated time spent
items.description: Displays the approximate accumulated elapsed time spent in compilation, in seconds.

items.name: Memory: Heap memory committed
items.description: Current heap memory allocated. This amount of memory is guaranteed for the Java virtual machine to use.

items.name: Memory: Heap memory maximum size
items.description: Maximum amount of heap that can be used for memory management. This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available if it is greater than the amount of committed memory. The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum size.

items.name: Memory: Heap memory used
items.description: Current memory usage outside the heap.

items.name: Memory: Non-Heap memory committed
items.description: Current memory allocated outside the heap. This amount of memory is guaranteed for the Java virtual machine to use.

items.name: Memory: Non-Heap memory maximum size
items.description: Maximum amount of non-heap memory that can be used for memory management. This amount of memory is not guaranteed to be available if it is greater than the amount of committed memory. The Java virtual machine may fail to allocate memory even if the amount of used memory does not exceed this maximum

items.name: Memory: Non-Heap memory used
items.description: Current memory usage outside the heap

items.name: Memory: Object pending finalization count
items.description: The approximate number of objects for which finalization is pending.

items.name: Threading: Total started thread count
items.description: The number of threads started since the JVM was launched.

ICMP loss

items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

item_prototypes.name: {#MODULE_NAME}: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HH3C-ENTITY-EXT-MIB
The CPU usage for this entity. Generally, the CPU usage
will calculate the overall CPU usage on the entity, and it
is not sensible with the number of CPU on the entity

item_prototypes.name: {#SNMPVALUE}: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HH3C-ENTITY-EXT-MIB
The temperature for the {#SNMPVALUE}.

item_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: Fan status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HH3C-ENTITY-EXT-MIB
Indicate the error state of this entity object.
fanError(41) means that the fan stops working.

item_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: Power supply status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HH3C-ENTITY-EXT-MIB
Indicate the error state of this entity object.
psuError(51) means that the Power Supply Unit is in the state of fault.
rpsError(61) means the Redundant Power Supply is in the state of fault.

The memory usage for the entity. This object indicates what
percent of memory are used.

One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:

An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n' then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000' to`n+499,999'.  For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accu

item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

item_prototypes.description: MIB: ENTITY-MIB

item_prototypes.description: MIB: ENTITY-MIB

item_prototypes.description: MIB: ENTITY-MIB

item_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: Temperature
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB
The temperature for the {#SNMPVALUE}.

item_prototypes.name: {#ENT_NAME}: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB
The CPU usage for this entity. Generally, the CPU usage will calculate the overall CPU usage on the entity, and itis not sensible with the number of CPU on the entity.
Reference: http://support.huawei.com/enterprise/KnowledgebaseReadAction.action?contentId=KB100009023

item_prototypes.description: MIB: ENTITY-MIB

MIB: HUAWEI-ENTITY-EXTENT-MIB
The memory usage for the entity. This object indicates what percent of memory are used.
Reference: http://support.huawei.com/enterprise/KnowledgebaseReadAction.action?contentId=KB1000090234

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of th

item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCoun

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of t

item_prototypes.description:

For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them fro

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n' then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000' to`n+499,999'.  For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accu

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Duplex status
item_prototypes.description:

The current mode of operation of the MAC

duplex mode could not be determined.

Management control of the duplex mode is
accomplished through the MAU MIB.  When

items.name: ICMP ping
ICMP loss
items.description: The percentage of lost packets.

items.name: ICMP response time
items.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

items.name: Total connection attempts
items.description: The total number of connections to the Web or FTP service that have been attempted since service startup. The count is the total for all Web sites or FTP sites combined.

items.name: Method POST requests per second
items.description: Rate of HTTP requests using POST method. Generally used for forms or gateway requests. Average per minute.

items.name: Method PROPFIND requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the PROPFIND method made. Propfind requests retrieve property values on files and directories. Average per minute.

items.name: Method PROPPATCH requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the PROPPATCH method made. Proppatch requests set property values on files and directories. Average per minute.

items.name: Method PUT requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the PUT method made. Average per minute.

items.name: Method MS-SEARCH requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the MS-SEARCH method made. Search requests are used to query the server to find resources that match a set of conditions provided by the client. Average per minute.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: The service uptime expressed in seconds.

items.name: Method Total requests per second
items.description: The rate of all HTTP requests received. Average per minute.

items.name: Method OPTIONS requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the OPTIONS method made. Average per minute.

items.name: Method TRACE requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the TRACE method made. Average per minute.

items.name: Method TRACE requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the UNLOCK method made. Unlock requests are used to remove locks from files. Average per minute.

items.name: Files cache hits percentage
items.description: The ratio of user-mode file cache hits to total cache requests (since service startup). Note: This value might be low if the Kernel URI cache hits percentage is high.

items.name: File cache misses
items.description: The total number of unsuccessful lookups in the user-mode file cache since service startup.

items.name: URIs cache hits percentage
items.description: The ratio of user-mode URI Cache Hits to total cache requests (since service startup)

items.name: URI cache misses
items.description: The total number of unsuccessful lookups in the user-mode URI cache since service startup.

items.name: World Wide Web Publishing Service (W3SVC) state
items.description: The World Wide Web Publishing Service (W3SVC) provides web connectivity and administration of websites through the IIS snap-in. If the World Wide Web Publishing Service stops, the operating system cannot serve any form of web request. This service was dependent on "Windows Process Activation Service

items.name: Method Total Other requests per second
items.description: Total Other Request Methods is the number of HTTP requests that are not OPTIONS, GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, TRACE, MOVE, COPY, MKCOL, PROPFIND, PROPPATCH, SEARCH, LOCK or UNLOCK methods (since service startup). Average per minute.

items.name: Not Found errors per second
items.description: The rate of errors due to requests that couldn't be satisfied by the server because the requested document could not be found. These are generally reported to the client with HTTP error code 404. Average per minute.

items.name: Anonymous users per second
items.description: The number of requests from users over an anonymous connection per second. Average per minute.

items.name: Current connections
items.description: The number of active connections.

items.name: Bytes Received per second
items.description: The average rate per minute at which data bytes are received by the service at the Application Layer. Does not include protocol headers or control bytes.

items.name: Bytes Sent per second
items.description: The average rate per minute at which data bytes are sent by the service.

items.name: Bytes Total per second
items.description: The average rate per minute of total bytes/sec transferred by the Web service (sum of bytes sent/sec and bytes received/sec).

items.name: Method CGI requests per second
items.description: The rate of CGI requests that are simultaneously being processed by the Web service. Average per minute.

items.name: Connection attempts per second
items.description: The average rate per minute that connections using the Web service are being attempted. The count is the average for all Web sites combined.

items.name: Method COPY requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests made using the COPY method. Copy requests are used for copying files and directories. Average per minute.

items.name: Method DELETE requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the DELETE method made. Average per minute.

items.name: NonAnonymous users per second
items.description: The number of requests from users over a non-anonymous connection per second. Average per minute.

items.name: Method GET requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests made using the GET method. GET requests are generally used for basic file retrievals or image maps, though they can be used with forms. Average per minute.

items.name: Method HEAD requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the HEAD method made. HEAD requests generally indicate a client is querying the state of a document they already have to see if it needs to be refreshed. Average per minute.

items.name: Method ISAPI requests per second
items.description: The rate of ISAPI Extension requests that are simultaneously being processed by the Web service. Average per minute.

items.name: Locked errors per second
items.description: The rate of errors due to requests that couldn't be satisfied by the server because the requested document was locked. These are generally reported as an HTTP 423 error code to the client. Average per minute.

items.name: Method LOCK requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests made using the LOCK method. Lock requests are used to lock a file for one user so that only that user can modify the file. Average per minute.

items.name: Method MKCOL requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the MKCOL method made. Mkcol requests are used to create directories on the server. Average per minute.

items.name: Method MOVE requests per second
items.description: The rate of HTTP requests using the MOVE method made. Move requests are used for moving files and directories. Average per minute.

Allocatable Memory.

'Allocatable' on a Kubernetes node is defined as the amount of compute resources that are available for pods. The scheduler does not over-subscribe 'Allocatable'. 'CPU', 'memory' and 'ephemeral-storage' are supported as of now.

'Allocatable' on a Kubernetes node is defined as the amount of compute resources that are available for pods. The scheduler does not over-subscribe 'Allocatable'. 'CPU', 'memory' and 'ephemeral-storage' are supported as of now.

items.name: ICMP ping
System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Memory utilization
items.description: Please note that memory utilization is a rough estimate, since memory available is calculated as free+buffers+cached, which is not 100% accurate, but the best we can get using SNMP.

items.name: Total memory
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Total memory expressed in bytes.

items.name: Free memory
items.description: MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB

items.name: Memory (cached)
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Memory used by the page cache and slabs (Cached and Slab in /proc/meminfo).

items.name: Memory (buffers)
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Memory used by kernel buffers (Buffers in /proc/meminfo).

items.name: Available memory
items.description: Please note that memory utilization is a rough estimate, since memory available is calculated as free+buffers+cached, which is not 100% accurate, but the best we can get using SNMP.

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: Total swap space
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
The total amount of swap space configured for this host.

items.name: Free swap space in %
items.description: The free space of the swap volume/file expressed in %.

items.name: Free swap space
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
The amount of swap space currently unused or available.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: ICMP loss
items.description: The percentage of lost packets.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: Context switches per second
items.description: The combined rate at which all processors on the computer are switched from one thread to another.

items.name: Interrupts per second
items.description: Number of interrupts processed.

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: ICMP response time
items.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

items.name: SNMP walk mounted filesystems
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Snmp walk through dskEntry table. Collected data used in filesystem lld and dependent item prototypes.

items.name: SNMP walk block devices
items.description: Block devices are discovered from UCD-DISKIO-MIB::diskIOTable (http://net-snmp.sourceforge.net/docs/mibs/ucdDiskIOMIB.html#diskIOTable).

items.name: SNMP walk system CPUs
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
Discovering system CPUs.

items.name: Linux: SNMP walk network interfaces
items.description: Discovering interfaces from IF-MIB.

items.name: Linux: SNMP walk EtherLike-MIB interfaces
items.description: Discovering interfaces from IF-MIB and EtherLike-MIB. Interfaces with `up(1)` Operational Status are discovered.

items.name: SNMP walk CPU load averages
items.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
SNMP walk through laTable. The collected data used in dependent CPU load average items.

items.description: Count the number of CPU cores by counting number of cores discovered in hrProcessorTable using LLD.

MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Time spent on running a virtual CPU for a guest operating system.

item_prototypes.name: CPU guest nice time
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Time spent on running a niced guest (a virtual CPU for guest operating systems under the control of the Linux kernel).

Time the CPU has spent doing nothing.

Time the CPU has spent servicing hardware interrupts.

item_prototypes.name: CPU nice time

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of th

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifInOctets. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCoun

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of t

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them fro

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. This object is a 64-bit version of ifOutOctets.Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n`, then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000` to `n+499,999`.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no ac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Duplex status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: EtherLike-MIB
The current mode of operation of the MAC entity. `unknown` indicates that the current duplex mode could not be determined.
Management control of the duplex mode is accomplished through the MAU MIB. When an interface does not support autonegotiation or when autonegotiation is not

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read rate
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-DISKIO-MIB
The number of read accesses from this device since boot.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk utilization
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-DISKIO-MIB
The 1 minute average load of disk (%)

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write rate
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-DISKIO-MIB
The number of write accesses from this device since boot.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Get data
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Intermediate data for subsequent processing.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Inodes: Free, in %
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Free metadata space expressed as percentage.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used, in %
item_prototypes.description:
UCD-SNMP-MIB::dskEntry
Space utilization is calculated as the percentage of currently used space compared to the maximum available space.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Total
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Total storage is calculated from two portions:
  dskTotalHigh
  dskTotalLow
Together they compose 64-bit number.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: UCD-SNMP-MIB
Used storage is calculated from two portions:
  dskUsedHigh
  dskUsedLow
Together they compose 64-bit number.
Reserved space is not counted in.

item_prototypes.name: CPU utilization
item_prototypes.description: CPU utilization expressed in %.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Available
item_prototypes.description:
UCD-SNMP-MIB::dskEntry
Available storage space is calculated from two portions:
  dskAvailHigh
  dskAvailLow
Together they compose 64-bit number.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}: Operational status
item_prototypes.description: Reference: https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/networking/operstates.txt

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}: Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
It indicates the interface protocol type as a decimal value.
See `include/uapi/linux/if_arp.h` for all possible values.
Reference: https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/ABI/testing/sysfs-class-net

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read request avg waiting time (r_await)
item_prototypes.description: This formula contains two Boolean expressions that evaluate to 1 or 0 in order to set the calculated metric to zero and to avoid the exception - division by zero.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write request avg waiting time (w_await)
item_prototypes.description: This formula contains two Boolean expressions that evaluate to 1 or 0 in order to set the calculated metric to zero and to avoid the exception - division by zero.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Get stats
item_prototypes.description: The contents of get `/sys/block/{#DEVNAME}/stat` to get the disk statistics.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}: Speed
item_prototypes.description:
It indicates the latest or current speed value of the interface. The value is an integer representing the link speed expressed in bits/sec.
This attribute is only valid for the interfaces that implement the ethtool `get_link_ksettings` method (mostly Ethernet).

Reference: https://www.kernel.org/

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk average queue size (avgqu-sz)
item_prototypes.description: The current average disk queue; the number of requests outstanding on the disk while the performance data is being collected.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read rate
item_prototypes.description: r/s (read operations per second) - the number (after merges) of read requests completed per second for the device.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read time (rate)
item_prototypes.description: The rate of total read time counter; used in `r_await` calculation.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk utilization
item_prototypes.description: The percentage of elapsed time during which the selected disk drive was busy while servicing read or write requests.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write rate
item_prototypes.description: w/s (write operations per second) - the number (after merges) of write requests completed per second for the device.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write time (rate)
item_prototypes.description: The rate of total write time counter; used in `w_await` calculation.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Get data
item_prototypes.description: Intermediate data of `{#FSNAME}` filesystem.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Inodes: Free, in %
item_prototypes.description: Free metadata space expressed in %.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used, in %
item_prototypes.description: Calculated as the percentage of currently used space compared to the maximum available space.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Total
item_prototypes.description: Total space expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used
item_prototypes.description: Used storage expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Option: Read-only
item_prototypes.description: The filesystem is mounted as read-only. It is available only for Zabbix agents 6.4 and higher.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Available
item_prototypes.description: Available storage space expressed in bytes.

Auto-parameterization occurs when an instance of SQL Server tries to parameterize a Transact-SQL request by replacing some literals with parameters so that reuse o

items.name: Average latch wait time base
items.description: For internal use only.

items.name: Average latch wait time raw
items.description: Average latch wait time (in milliseconds) for latch requests that had to wait.

items.name: Total average wait time base
items.description: For internal use only.

items.name: Total average wait time raw
items.description: Average amount of wait time (in milliseconds) for each lock request that resulted in a wait. Information for all locks.

items.name: Batch requests per second
items.description: Number of Transact-SQL command batches received per second. This statistic is affected by all constraints (such as I/O, number of users, cache size, complexity of requests, and so on). High batch requests mean good throughput.

items.name: Buffer cache hit ratio
items.description: Indicates the percentage of pages found in the buffer cache without having to read from the disk. The ratio is the total number of cache hits divided by the total number of cache lookups over the last few thousand page accesses. After a long period of time, the ratio changes very little. Since readi

items.name: Cache hit ratio
items.description: Ratio between cache hits and lookups.

items.name: Cache objects in use
items.description: Number of cache objects in use.

items.name: Cache object counts
items.description: Number of cache objects in the cache.

items.name: Cache pages
items.description: Number of 8-kilobyte (KB) pages used by cache objects.

items.name: Checkpoint pages per second
items.description: Indicates the number of pages flushed to the disk per second by a checkpoint or other operation which required all dirty pages to be flushed.

items.name: Database pages
items.description: Indicates the number of pages in the buffer pool with database content.

items.name: Total data file size
items.description: Total size of all data files.

items.name: Total errors per second
items.description: Number of errors per second.

items.name: Failed auto-params per second
items.description: Number of failed auto-parameterization attempts per second. This number should be small. Note that auto-parameterizations are also known as simple parameterizations in the newer versions of SQL Server.

items.name: Forwarded records per second
items.description: Number of records per second fetched through forwarded record pointers.

items.name: Free list stalls per second
items.description: Indicates the number of requests per second that had to wait for a free page.

items.name: Full scans per second
items.description: Number of unrestricted full scans per second. These can be either base-table or full-index scans. Values greater than 1 or 2 indicate that there are table / index page scans. If that is combined with high CPU, this counter requires further investigation, otherwise, if the full scans are on small tab

items.name: Granted Workspace Memory
items.description: Specifies the total amount of memory currently granted to executing processes, such as hash, sort, bulk copy, and index creation operations.

items.name: Index searches per second
items.description: Number of index searches per second. These are used to start a range scan, reposition a range scan, revalidate a scan point, fetch a single index record, and search down the index to locate where to insert a new row.

items.name: Errors per second (Info errors)
items.description: Number of errors per second.

items.name: Errors per second (Kill connection errors)
items.description: Number of errors per second.

items.name: Latch waits per second
items.description: The number of latch requests that could not be granted immediately. Latches are lightweight means of holding a very transient server resource, such as an address in memory.

items.name: Lazy writes per second
items.description: Indicates the number of buffers written per second by the buffer manager's lazy writer. The lazy writer is a system process that flushes out batches of dirty, aged buffers (buffers that contain changes that must be written back to the disk before the buffer can be reused for a different page) and ma

items.name: Total lock requests per second
items.description: Number of new locks and lock conversions per second requested from the lock manager.

items.name: Total lock requests per second that timed out
items.description: Number of timed out lock requests per second, including requests for NOWAIT locks.

items.name: Total lock requests per second that required waiting
items.description: Number of lock requests per second that required the caller to wait.

items.name: Lock wait time
items.description: Average of total wait time (in milliseconds) for locks in the last second.

items.name: Logins per second
items.description: Total number of logins started per second. This does not include pooled connections. Any value over 2 may indicate insufficient connection pooling.

items.name: Logouts per second
items.description: Total number of logout operations started per second. Any value over 2 may indicate insufficient connection pooling.

items.name: Total log file size
items.description: Total size of all the transaction log files.

items.name: Total log file used size
items.description: The cumulative size of all the log files in the database.

items.name: Maximum workspace memory
items.description: Indicates the maximum amount of memory available for executing processes, such as hash, sort, bulk copy, and index creation operations.

items.name: Memory grants outstanding
items.description: Specifies the total number of processes that have successfully acquired a workspace memory grant.

items.name: Memory grants pending
items.description: Specifies the total number of processes waiting for a workspace memory grant.

items.name: Total lock requests per second that have deadlocks
items.description: Number of lock requests per second that resulted in a deadlock.

items.name: Errors per second (DB offline errors)
items.description: Number of errors per second.

items.name: Page life expectancy
items.description: Indicates the number of seconds a page will stay in the buffer pool without references.

items.name: Page lookups per second
items.description: Indicates the number of requests per second to find a page in the buffer pool.

items.name: Page reads per second
items.description: Indicates the number of physical database page reads that are issued per second. This statistic displays the total number of physical page reads across all databases. As physical I/O is expensive, you may be able to minimize the cost either by using a larger data cache, intelligent indexes, and more

items.name: Page splits per second
items.description: Number of page splits per second that occur as a result of overflowing index pages.

items.name: Page writes per second
items.description: Indicates the number of physical database page writes that are issued per second.

items.name: Number of blocked processes
items.description: Number of currently blocked processes.

items.name: Read-ahead pages per second
items.description: Indicates the number of pages read per second in anticipation of use.

items.name: Safe auto-params per second
items.description: Number of safe auto-parameterization attempts per second. Safe refers to a determination that a cached execution plan can be shared between different similar-looking Transact-SQL statements. SQL Server makes many auto-parameterization attempts, some of which turn out to be safe and others fail. Note

items.name: SQL compilations per second
items.description: Number of SQL compilations per second. Indicates the number of times the compile code path is entered. Includes runs caused by statement-level recompilations in SQL Server. After SQL Server user activity is stable, this value reaches a steady state.

items.name: SQL re-compilations per second
items.description: Number of statement recompiles per second. Counts the number of times statement recompiles are triggered. Generally, you want the recompiles to be low.

items.name: Table lock escalations per second
items.description: Number of times locks on a table were escalated to the TABLE or HoBT granularity.

items.name: Target pages
items.description: The optimal number of pages in the buffer pool.

items.name: Target server memory
items.description: Indicates the ideal amount of memory the server can consume.

items.name: Total latch wait time
items.description: Total latch wait time (in milliseconds) for latch requests in the last second. This value should stay stable compared to the number of latch waits per second.

items.name: Total server memory
items.description: Specifies the amount of memory the server has committed using the memory manager.

items.name: Total transactions per second
items.description: Total number of transactions started for all databases per second.

items.name: Unsafe auto-params per second
items.description: Number of unsafe auto-parameterization attempts per second. For example, the query has some characteristics that prevent the cached plan from being shared. These are designated as unsafe. This does not count the number of forced parameterizations.

items.name: Number of users connected
items.description: Number of users connected to MSSQL Server.

items.name: Errors per second (User errors)
items.description: Number of errors per second.

items.name: Work files created per second
items.description: Number of work files created per second. For example, work files can be used to store temporary results for hash joins and hash aggregates.

items.name: Work tables created per second
items.description: Number of work tables created per second. For example, work tables can be used to store temporary results for query spool, LOB variables, XML variables, and cursors.

items.name: Worktables from cache ratio
items.description: Percentage of work tables created where the initial two pages of the work table were not allocated but were immediately available from the work table cache.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}': Primary replica recovery health
item_prototypes.description:
Indicates the recovery health of the primary replica:
0 = In progress
1 = Online
2 = Unavailable

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}': Primary replica name
item_prototypes.description: Name of the server instance that is hosting the current primary replica.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}': Secondary replica recovery health
item_prototypes.description:
Indicates the recovery health of a secondary replica:
0 = In progress
1 = Online
2 = Unavailable

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}': Synchronization health
item_prototypes.description:
Reflects a rollup of the `synchronization_health` of all availability replicas in the availability group:
0 = Not healthy. None of the availability replicas have a healthy synchronization.
1 = Partially healthy. The synchronization of some, but not all, availability replicas is healthy.
2 = Healt

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Get last backup
item_prototypes.description: The item gets information about backup processes for {#DBNAME}.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Get performance counters
item_prototypes.description: The item gets server status information for {#DBNAME}.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Get job status
item_prototypes.description: The item gets the status of SQL agent job {#JOBNAME}.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Local DB '{#DBNAME}': Suspended
item_prototypes.description:
Database state:
0 = Resumed
1 = Suspended

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Local DB '{#DBNAME}': State
item_prototypes.description:
0 = Online
1 = Restoring
2 = Recovering
3 = Recovery pending
4 = Suspect
5 = Emergency
6 = Offline

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Local DB '{#DBNAME}': Synchronization health
item_prototypes.description:
Reflects the intersection of the synchronization state of a database that is joined to the availability group on the availability replica and the availability mode of the availability replica (synchronous-commit or asynchronous-commit mode):
0 = Not healthy. The synchronization_state of the databas

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Mirroring '{#DBNAME}': Role
item_prototypes.description:
Current role of the local database plays in the database mirroring session.
1 = Principal
2 = Mirror

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Mirroring '{#DBNAME}': Role sequence
item_prototypes.description: The number of times that mirroring partners have switched the principal and mirror roles due to a failover or forced service.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Mirroring '{#DBNAME}': Safety level
item_prototypes.description:
Safety setting for updates on the mirror database:
0 = Unknown state
1 = Off [asynchronous]
2 = Full [synchronous]

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Mirroring '{#DBNAME}': State
item_prototypes.description:
State of the mirror database and of the database mirroring session.
0 = Suspended
1 = Disconnected from the other partner
2 = Synchronizing
3 = Pending failover
4 = Synchronized
5 = The partners are not synchronized. Failover is not possible now.
6 = The partners are synchronized. Failover is

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Mirroring '{#DBNAME}': Witness state
item_prototypes.description:
State of the witness in the database mirroring session of the database:
0 = Unknown
1 = Connected
2 = Disconnected

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Non-Local DB '*{#REPLICA_NAME}*{#DBNAME}': Log queue size
item_prototypes.description: Amount of the log records of the primary database that has not been sent to the secondary databases.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Non-Local DB '*{#REPLICA_NAME}*{#DBNAME}': Redo log queue size
item_prototypes.description: Amount of log records in the log files of the secondary replica that has not yet been redone.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Cluster '{#CLUSTER_NAME}': Quorum state
item_prototypes.description:
State of the WSFC quorum, one of:
0 = Unknown quorum state
1 = Normal quorum
2 = Forced quorum

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Cluster '{#CLUSTER_NAME}': Quorum type
item_prototypes.description:
Type of quorum used by this WSFC cluster, one of:
0 = Node Majority. This quorum configuration can sustain failures of half the nodes (rounding up) minus one.
1 = Node and Disk Majority. If the disk witness remains on line, this quorum configuration can sustain failures of half the nodes (rounding

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Cluster member '{#MEMBER_NAME}': Member state
item_prototypes.description:
The member state, one of:
0 = Offline
1 = Online

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Cluster member '{#MEMBER_NAME}': Member type
item_prototypes.description:
The type of member, one of:
0 = WSFC node
1 = Disk witness
2 = File share witness
3 = Cloud Witness

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Cluster member '{#MEMBER_NAME}': Number of quorum votes
item_prototypes.description: Number of quorum votes possessed by this quorum member.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Connected state
item_prototypes.description:
Whether a secondary replica is currently connected to the primary replica:
0 = Disconnected. The response of an availability replica to the "Disconnected" state depends on its role:
On the primary replica, if a secondary replica is disconnected, its secondary databases are marked as "Not synchroni

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Is local
item_prototypes.description:
Whether the replica is local:
0 = Indicates a remote secondary replica in an availability group whose primary replica is hosted by the local server instance. This value occurs only on the primary replica location.
1 = Indicates a local replica. On secondary replicas, this is the only available val

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Join state
item_prototypes.description:
0 = Not joined
1 = Joined, standalone instance
2 = Joined, failover cluster instance

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Operational state
item_prototypes.description:
Current operational state of the replica:
0 = Pending failover
1 = Pending
2 = Online
3 = Offline
4 = Failed
5 = Failed, no quorum
6 = Not local

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Recovery health
item_prototypes.description:
Rollup of the "database_state" column of the `sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states` dynamic management view:
0 = In progress. At least one joined database has a database state other than "Online" (database_state is not "0").
1 = Online. All the joined databases have a database state of "Online" (da

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Role
item_prototypes.description:
Current Always On availability group role of a local replica or a connected remote replica:
0 = Resolving
1 = Primary
2 = Secondary

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL AG '{#GROUP_NAME}' Replica '{#REPLICA_NAME}': Sync health
item_prototypes.description: Reflects a rollup of the database synchronization state (synchronization_state) of all joined availability databases (also known as replicas) and the availability mode of the replica (synchronous-commit or asynchronous-commit mode). The rollup will reflect the least healthy accumulated state of the

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last diff backup duration
item_prototypes.description: Duration of the last differential backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last diff backup (time ago)
item_prototypes.description: The amount of time since the last differential backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last full backup duration
item_prototypes.description: Duration of the last full backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last full backup (time ago)
item_prototypes.description: The amount of time since the last full backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last log backup duration
item_prototypes.description: Duration of the last log backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Last log backup (time ago)
item_prototypes.description: The amount of time since the last log backup.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Recovery model
item_prototypes.description:
Recovery model selected:
1 = Full
2 = Bulk_logged
3 = Simple

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Active transactions
item_prototypes.description: Number of active transactions for the database.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Data file size
item_prototypes.description: Cumulative size of all the data files in the database including any automatic growth. Monitoring this counter is useful, for example, for determining the correct size of `tempdb`.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log bytes flushed per second
item_prototypes.description: Total number of log bytes flushed per second. Useful for determining trends and utilization of the transaction log.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log file size
item_prototypes.description: Cumulative size of all the transaction log files in the database.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log file used size
item_prototypes.description: Cumulative size of all the log files in the database.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log flushes per second
item_prototypes.description: Number of log flushes per second.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log flush waits per second
item_prototypes.description: Number of commits per second waiting for the log flush.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log flush wait time
item_prototypes.description: Total wait time (in milliseconds) to flush the log. On an Always On secondary database, this value indicates the wait time for log records to be hardened to disk.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log growths
item_prototypes.description: Total number of times the transaction log for the database has been expanded.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log shrinks
item_prototypes.description: Total number of times the transaction log for the database has been shrunk.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Log truncations
item_prototypes.description: Number of times the transaction log has been shrunk.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Percent log used
item_prototypes.description: Percentage of log space in use.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': State
item_prototypes.description:
0 = Online
1 = Restoring
2 = Recovering | SQL Server 2008 and later
3 = Recovery_pending | SQL Server 2008 and later
4 = Suspect
5 = Emergency | SQL Server 2008 and later
6 = Offline | SQL Server 2008 and later
7 = Copying | Azure SQL Database Active Geo-Replication
10 = Offline_secondary |

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL DB '{#DBNAME}': Transactions per second
item_prototypes.description: Number of transactions started for the database per second.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Enabled
item_prototypes.description:
The possible values of the job status:
0 = Disabled
1 = Enabled

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Last run date-time
item_prototypes.description: The last date-time of the job run.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Last run status message
item_prototypes.description: An informational message about the last run of the job.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Next run date-time
item_prototypes.description: The next date-time of the job run.

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Run status
item_prototypes.description:
The possible values of the job status:
0 ⇒ Failed
1 ⇒ Succeeded
2 ⇒ Retry
3 ⇒ Canceled
4 ⇒ Running

item_prototypes.name: MSSQL Job '{#JOBNAME}': Run duration
item_prototypes.description: Duration of the last-run job.

lld_rules.description: Scanning databases in DBMS.

lld_rules.description: Discovery of the local availability databases.

To see non-NULL values on a mirror database, you must be a member of

lld_rules.description: Discovery of the non-local (not local to SQL Server instance) availability databases.

lld_rules.description: Discovery of the quorum members of the WSFC cluster.
