items.name: Commands: FLUSH per second
items.description: The flush_all command invalidates all items in the database. This operation incurs a performance penalty and shouldn't take place in production, so check your debug scripts.

items.name: Bytes used
items.description: Current number of bytes used to store items.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: Number of seconds since Memcached server start

items.name: New items per second
items.description: Number of new items stored per second.

items.name: Threads
items.description: Number of worker threads requested

items.name: Misses per second
items.description: Number of missed GET requests (items requested but not found) per second.

items.name: Hits per second
items.description: Number of successful GET requests (items requested and found) per second.

items.name: Evictions per second
items.description:
"An eviction is when an item that still has time to live is removed from the cache because a brand new item needs to be allocated.
The item is selected with a pseudo-LRU mechanism.
A high number of evictions coupled with a low hit rate means your application is setting a large number of keys that

items.name: Current number of items stored
items.description: Current number of items stored by this instance.

items.name: Written bytes per second
items.description: The network's read rate per second in B/sec

items.name: Read bytes per second
items.description: The network's read rate per second in B/sec

items.name: Process id
items.description: PID of the server process

items.name: Commands: GET per second
items.description: Number of GET requests received by server per second.

items.name: CPU user
items.description: User CPU consumed by the Memcached server

items.name: CPU sys
items.description: System CPU consumed by the Memcached server

items.name: Throttled connections
items.description: Number of times a client connection was throttled. When sending GETs in batch mode and the connection contains too many requests (limited by -R parameter) the connection might be throttled to prevent starvation.

items.name: Connection structures
items.description: Number of  connection structures allocated by the server

items.name: New connections per second
items.description: Number of connections opened per second

items.name: Queued connections per second
items.description: Number of times that memcached has hit its connections limit and disabled its listener

items.name: Max connections
items.description: Max number of concurrent connections

items.name: Open connections
items.description: The number of clients presently connected

items.name: Maximum number of bytes
items.description: Maximum number of bytes allowed in cache. You can adjust this setting via a config file or the command line while starting your Memcached server.

items.name: Commands: SET per second
items.description: Number of SET requests received by server per second.

items.name: Memcached version
items.description: Version of the Memcached server

items.name: MongoDB version
items.description: Version of the MongoDB server.

items.name: Get collections usage stats
items.description: Returns usage statistics for each collection.

items.name: Get server status
items.description: Returns a database's state.

items.name: Get Replica Set status
items.description: Returns the replica set status from the point of view of the member where the method is run.

items.name: Ping
items.description: Test if a connection is alive or not.

items.name: Get oplog stats
items.description: Returns status of the replica set, using data polled from the oplog.

items.name: Document: deleted, rate
items.description: Number of documents deleted per second.

items.name: Requests, rate
items.description: Number of distinct requests that the server has received per second

items.name: Memory: mapped with journal
items.description: The amount of mapped memory, including the memory used for journaling.

items.name: Memory: resident
items.description: Amount of memory currently used by the database process.

items.name: Memory: virtual
items.description: Amount of virtual memory used by the mongod process.

items.name: Cursor: open no timeout
items.description: Number of open cursors with the option DBQuery.Option.noTimeout set to prevent timeout after a period of inactivity.

items.name: Bytes in, rate
items.description: The total number of bytes that the server has received over network connections initiated by clients or other mongod/mongos instances per second.

items.name: Bytes out, rate
items.description: The total number of bytes that the server has sent over network connections initiated by clients or other mongod/mongos instances per second.

items.name: Operations: delete, rate
items.description: The number of delete operations the mongod instance per second.

items.name: Operations: command, rate
items.description:
The number of commands issued to the database the mongod instance per second.
Counts all commands except the write commands: insert, update, and delete.

items.name: Architecture
items.description: A number, either 64 or 32, that indicates whether the MongoDB instance is compiled for 64-bit or 32-bit architecture.

items.name: Operations: getmore, rate
items.description:
The number of "getmore" operations since the mongod instance per second. This counter can be high even if the query count is low.
Secondary nodes send getMore operations as part of the replication process.

items.name: Operations: insert, rate
items.description: The number of insert operations received since the mongod instance per second.

items.name: Operations: query, rate
items.description: The number of queries received the mongod instance per second.

items.name: Operations: update, rate
items.description: The number of update operations the mongod instance per second.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: Number of seconds that the mongod process has been active.

items.name: Memory: mapped
items.description: Amount of mapped memory by the database.

items.name: Cursor: timed out, rate
items.description: Number of cursors that time out, per second.

items.name: Document: inserted, rate
items.description: Number of documents inserted per second.

items.name: Asserts: user, rate
items.description:
The number of "user asserts" that have occurred per second.
These are errors that user may generate, such as out of disk space or duplicate key.

items.name: Document: returned, rate
items.description: Number of documents returned by queries per second.

items.name: Document: updated, rate
items.description: Number of documents updated per second.

items.name: Active clients: readers
items.description: The number of the active client connections performing read operations.

items.name: Active clients: total
items.description: The total number of internal client connections to the database including system threads as well as queued readers and writers.

items.name: Active clients: writers
items.description: The number of active client connections performing write operations.

items.name: Asserts: message, rate
items.description:
The number of message assertions raised per second.
Check the log file for more information about these messages.

items.name: Asserts: regular, rate
items.description:
The number of regular assertions raised per second.
Check the log file for more information about these messages.

items.name: Asserts: rollovers, rate
items.description:
Number of times that the rollover counters roll over per second.
The counters rollover to zero every 2^30 assertions.

items.name: Asserts: warning, rate
items.description: The number of warnings raised per second.

items.name: Cursor: open total
items.description: Number of cursors that MongoDB is maintaining for clients.

items.name: Connections, active
items.description:
The number of active client connections to the server.
Active client connections refers to client connections that currently have operations in progress.
Available starting in  4.0.7, 0 for older versions.

items.name: Connections, available
items.description: The number of unused incoming connections available.

items.name: Connections, current
items.description:
The number of incoming connections from clients to the database server.
This number includes the current shell session.

items.name: New connections, rate
items.description: Rate of all incoming connections created to the server.

items.name: Current queue: readers
items.description:
The number of operations that are currently queued and waiting for the read lock.
A consistently small read-queue, particularly of shorter operations, should cause no concern.

items.name: Current queue: total
items.description: The total number of operations queued waiting for the lock.

items.name: Current queue: writers
items.description:
The number of operations that are currently queued and waiting for the write lock.
 A consistently small write-queue, particularly of shorter operations, is no cause for concern.

items.name: Cursor: open pinned
items.description: Number of pinned open cursors.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Get collection stats {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}
item_prototypes.description: Returns a variety of storage statistics for a given collection.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Get db stats {#DBNAME}
item_prototypes.description: Returns statistics reflecting the database system's state.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Objects, avg size
item_prototypes.description: The size of the average object in the collection in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: remove, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Write lock, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Storage size
item_prototypes.description: Total storage space allocated to this collection for document storage.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Size
item_prototypes.description: The total size in bytes of the data in the collection plus the size of every indexes on the mongodb.collection.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Read lock, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Read lock, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: update, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: update, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: total, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: total, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: remove, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: queries, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Capped
item_prototypes.description: Whether or not the collection is capped.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: queries, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: insert, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: insert, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: getmore, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: getmore, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: commands, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Operations: commands, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent to operations.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Indexes
item_prototypes.description: Total number of indices on the collection.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Capped: max size
item_prototypes.description: Maximum size of a capped collection in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Capped: max number
item_prototypes.description: Maximum number of documents that may be present in a capped collection.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Objects, count
item_prototypes.description: Total number of objects in the collection.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}.{#COLLECTION}: Write lock, rate
item_prototypes.description: The number of operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Collections
item_prototypes.description: Contains a count of the number of collections in that database.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Size, data
item_prototypes.description: Total size of the data held in this database including the padding factor.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Extents
item_prototypes.description: Contains a count of the number of extents in the database across all collections.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Size, file
item_prototypes.description: Total size of the data held in this database including the padding factor (only available with the mmapv1 storage engine).

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Size, index
item_prototypes.description: Total size of all indexes created on this database.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Objects, count
item_prototypes.description: Number of objects (documents) in the database across all collections.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Objects, avg size
item_prototypes.description: The average size of each document in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#DBNAME}: Size, storage
item_prototypes.description: Total amount of space allocated to collections in this database for document storage.

item_prototypes.name: Apply batches, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) the mongod has spent applying operations from the oplog.

item_prototypes.name: Network readers created, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of oplog query processes created per second.

item_prototypes.name: Unhealthy replicas
item_prototypes.description: The replicated nodes in current ReplicaSet with member health value  = 0.

item_prototypes.name: Number of replicas
item_prototypes.description: The number of replicated nodes in current ReplicaSet.

item_prototypes.name: Node state
item_prototypes.description: An integer between 0 and 10 that represents the replica state of the current member.

item_prototypes.name: Preload indexes, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of index entries loaded by members before updating documents as part of the pre-fetch stage of replication.

item_prototypes.name: Preload indexes, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) spent loading documents as part of the pre-fetch stage of replication.

item_prototypes.name: Preload docs, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of documents loaded per second during the pre-fetch stage of replication.

item_prototypes.name: Preload docs, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) spent loading documents as part of the pre-fetch stage of replication.

item_prototypes.name: MongoDB {#RS_NAME}: Oplog time diff
item_prototypes.description: Oplog window: difference between the first and last operation in the oplog. Only present if there are entries in the oplog.

item_prototypes.name: Network ops, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of operations read from the replication source per second.

item_prototypes.name: Apply batches, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of batches applied across all databases per second.

item_prototypes.name: Network getmores, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of getmore operations per second.

item_prototypes.name: Network getmores, ms/s
item_prototypes.description: Fraction of time (ms/s) required to collect data from getmore operations.

item_prototypes.name: Network bytes, rate
item_prototypes.description: Amount of data read from the replication sync source per second.

item_prototypes.name: Replication lag
item_prototypes.description: Delay between a write operation on the primary and its copy to a secondary.

item_prototypes.name: Buffer, size
item_prototypes.description: Current size of the contents of the oplog buffer.

item_prototypes.name: Buffer, max size
item_prototypes.description: Maximum size of the buffer.

item_prototypes.name: Buffer
item_prototypes.description: Number of operations in the oplog buffer.

item_prototypes.name: Apply ops, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of oplog operations applied per second.

item_prototypes.name: Number of unhealthy replicas
item_prototypes.description: The number of replicated nodes with member health value  = 0.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: bytes
item_prototypes.description: Size of the data currently in cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: tracked dirty bytes in the cache
item_prototypes.description: Size of the dirty data in the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: write, out
item_prototypes.description: Number of write tickets (concurrent transactions) in use.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: write, available
item_prototypes.description: Number of available write tickets (concurrent transactions) remaining.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: read, total tickets
item_prototypes.description: Total number of read tickets (concurrent transactions) available.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: read, out
item_prototypes.description: Number of read tickets (concurrent transactions) in use.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: read, available
item_prototypes.description: Number of available read tickets (concurrent transactions) remaining.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: unmodified pages evicted
item_prototypes.description: Number of pages, that were not modified, evicted from the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: in-memory page splits
item_prototypes.description: In-memory page splits.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: bytes, max
item_prototypes.description: Maximum cache size.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: pages written from cache
item_prototypes.description: Number of pages written from the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: pages read into cache
item_prototypes.description: Number of pages read into the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: pages held in cache
item_prototypes.description: Number of pages currently held in the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: pages evicted by application threads, rate
item_prototypes.description: Number of page evicted by application threads per second.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: modified pages evicted
item_prototypes.description: Number of pages, that have been modified, evicted from the cache.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger cache: max page size at eviction
item_prototypes.description: Maximum page size at eviction.

item_prototypes.name: WiredTiger concurrent transactions: write, total tickets
item_prototypes.description: Total number of write tickets (concurrent transactions) available.

lld_rules.name: Collection discovery
lld_rules.description:
Collect collections metrics.
Note, depending on the number of DBs and collections this discovery operation may be expensive. Use filters with macros {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.DB.MATCHES}, {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.DB.NOT_MATCHES}, {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.COLLECTION.MATCHES}, {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.COLLECTION.NOT_

lld_rules.name: Database discovery
lld_rules.description:
Collect database metrics.
Note, depending on the number of DBs this discovery operation may be expensive. Use filters with macros {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.DB.MATCHES}, {$MONGODB.LLD.FILTER.DB.NOT_MATCHES}.

lld_rules.name: Replication discovery
lld_rules.description: Collect metrics by Zabbix agent if it exists.

lld_rules.name: WiredTiger metrics
lld_rules.description: Collect metrics of WiredTiger Storage Engine if it exists.

items.name: Version
items.description: MySQL server version.

items.name: Get status variables
items.description: Gets server global status information.

items.name: Status
items.description: MySQL server status.

items.name: Buffer pool efficiency
items.description: The item shows how effectively the buffer pool is serving reads.

items.name: Buffer pool utilization
items.description: Ratio of used to total pages in the buffer pool.

items.name: Calculated value of innodb_log_file_size
items.description: `Innodb_log_file_size` is calculated as: (`innodb_os_log_written`-`innodb_os_log_written`(time shift -1h))/`{$MYSQL.INNODB_LOG_FILES}`. `Innodb_log_file_size` is the size in bytes of the each InnoDB redo log file in the log group. The combined size can be no more than 512 GB. Larger values mean less

items.name: Get database
items.description: Used for scanning databases in DBMS.

items.name: Get replication
items.description: Gets replication status information.

items.name: InnoDB row lock waits
items.description: Number of times operations on InnoDB tables had to wait for a row lock.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool read requests
items.description: Number of logical read requests.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool read requests per second
items.description: Number of logical read requests per second.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool reads
items.description: Number of logical reads that InnoDB could not satisfy from the buffer pool and had to read directly from the disk.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool reads per second
items.description: Number of logical reads per second that InnoDB could not satisfy from the buffer pool and had to read directly from the disk.

items.name: InnoDB row lock time
items.description: The total time spent in acquiring row locks for InnoDB tables, in milliseconds.

items.name: InnoDB row lock time max
items.description: The maximum time to acquire a row lock for InnoDB tables, in milliseconds.

items.name: Queries per second
items.description: Number of statements executed by the server. This variable includes statements executed within stored programs, unlike the `Questions` variable.

items.name: Max used connections
items.description: The maximum number of connections that have been in use simultaneously since the server start.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool pages free
items.description: The total size of the InnoDB buffer pool, in pages.

items.name: Questions per second
items.description: Number of statements executed by the server. This includes only statements sent to the server by clients and not statements executed within stored programs, unlike the `Queries` variable.

items.name: Slow queries per second
items.description: Number of queries that have taken more than `long_query_time` seconds.

items.name: Threads cached
items.description: Number of threads in the thread cache.

items.name: Threads connected
items.description: Number of currently open connections.

items.name: Threads running
items.description: Number of threads that are not sleeping.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool pages total
items.description: The total size of the InnoDB buffer pool, in pages.

items.name: Command Select per second
items.description: The `Com_select` counter variable indicates the number of times the `SELECT` statement has been executed.

items.name: Aborted clients per second
items.description: Number of connections that were aborted because the client died without closing the connection properly.

items.name: Aborted connections per second
items.description: Number of failed attempts to connect to the MySQL server.

items.name: Bytes received
items.description: Number of bytes received from all clients.

items.name: Bytes sent
items.description: Number of bytes sent to all clients.

items.name: Command Delete per second
items.description: The `Com_delete` counter variable indicates the number of times the `DELETE` statement has been executed.

items.name: Command Insert per second
items.description: The `Com_insert` counter variable indicates the number of times the `INSERT` statement has been executed.

items.name: Command Update per second
items.description: The `Com_update` counter variable indicates the number of times the `UPDATE` statement has been executed.

items.name: Connection errors accept per second
items.description: Number of errors that occurred during calls to `accept()` on the listening port.

items.name: Connection errors internal per second
items.description: Number of refused connections due to internal server errors, for example, out of memory errors, or failed thread starts.

items.name: Connection errors max connections per second
items.description: Number of refused connections due to the `max_connections` limit being reached.

items.name: Connection errors peer address per second
items.description: Number of errors while searching for the connecting client's IP address.

items.name: Connection errors select per second
items.description: Number of errors during calls to `select()` or `poll()` on the listening port. The client would not necessarily have been rejected in these cases.

items.name: Connection errors tcpwrap per second
items.description: Number of connections the libwrap library has refused.

items.name: Connections per second
items.description: Number of connection attempts (successful or not) to the MySQL server.

items.name: Uptime
items.description: Number of seconds that the server has been up.

items.name: Threads created per second
items.description: Number of threads created to handle connections. If the value of `Threads_created` is large, you may want to increase the `thread_cache_size` value. The cache miss rate can be calculated as `Threads_created`/`Connections`.

items.name: Created tmp tables on disk per second
items.description: Number of internal on-disk temporary tables created by the server while executing statements.

items.name: Created tmp tables on memory per second
items.description: Number of internal temporary tables created by the server while executing statements.

items.name: Created tmp files on disk per second
items.description: How many temporary files `mysqld` has created.

items.name: Innodb buffer pool wait free
items.description: Number of times InnoDB waited for a free page before reading or creating a page. Normally, writes to the InnoDB buffer pool happen in the background. When no clean pages are available, dirty pages are flushed first in order to free some up. This counts the numbers of wait for this operation to finis

items.name: Innodb number open files
items.description: Number of open files held by InnoDB. InnoDB only.

items.name: Innodb log written
items.description: Number of bytes written to the InnoDB log.

items.name: Open tables
items.description: Number of tables that are open.

items.name: Open table definitions
items.description: Number of cached table definitions.

items.name: Binlog cache disk use
items.description: Number of transactions that used a temporary disk cache because they could not fit in the regular binary log cache, being larger than `binlog_cache_size`.

item_prototypes.name: Size of database {#DATABASE}
item_prototypes.description: Database size.

item_prototypes.name: Replication {#REPLICA.NAME} status
item_prototypes.description: Gets status information on the essential parameters of the {#REPLICA.KEY} threads.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Seconds Behind {#SOURCE.NAME}
item_prototypes.description: The number of seconds the {#REPLICA.KEY} SQL thread has been behind processing the {#SOURCE.KEY} binary log. A high number (or an increasing one) can indicate that the {#REPLICA.KEY} is unable to handle events from the {#SOURCE.KEY} in a timely fashion.

item_prototypes.name: Replication {#REPLICA.NAME} IO Running
item_prototypes.description: Whether the I/O thread for reading the {#SOURCE.KEY}'s binary log is running. Normally, you want this to be `Yes` unless you have not yet started a replication or have explicitly stopped it with `stop {#REPLICA.KEY}`.

item_prototypes.name: Replication {#REPLICA.NAME} SQL Running
item_prototypes.description:
Whether the SQL thread for executing events in the relay log is running.
As with the I/O thread, this should normally be `Yes`.

item_prototypes.name: Replication {#REPLICA.NAME} SQL Running State
item_prototypes.description: Shows the state of the SQL driver threads.

item_prototypes.name: Binlog commits
item_prototypes.description: Total number of transactions committed to the binary log.

item_prototypes.name: Binlog group commits
item_prototypes.description: Total number of group commits done to the binary log.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait count
item_prototypes.description: The number of times `MASTER_GTID_WAIT` called.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait timeouts
item_prototypes.description: Number of timeouts occurring in `MASTER_GTID_WAIT`.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait time
item_prototypes.description: Total number of time spent in `MASTER_GTID_WAIT`.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Seconds Behind Master {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description:
The number of seconds that the slave SQL thread is behind processing the master binary log.
A high number (or an increasing one) can indicate that the slave is unable to handle events
from the master in a timely fashion.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Slave IO Running {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description:
Whether the I/O thread for reading the master's binary log is running.
Normally, you want this to be `Yes` unless you have not yet started replication or have
explicitly stopped it with `STOP SLAVE`.

item_prototypes.description:

As with the I/O thread, this should normally be `Yes`.

items.description: Ratio of used to total pages in the buffer pool.

items.name: Buffer pool efficiency
items.description: The item shows how effectively the buffer pool is serving reads.

items.name: Calculated value of innodb_log_file_size
items.description: `Innodb_log_file_size` is calculated as: (`innodb_os_log_written`-`innodb_os_log_written`(time shift -1h))/`{$MYSQL.INNODB_LOG_FILES}`. `Innodb_log_file_size` is the size in bytes of the each InnoDB redo log file in the log group. The combined size can be no more than 512 GB. Larger values mean less

items.name: Uptime
items.description: Number of seconds that the server has been up.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool reads
items.description: Number of logical reads that InnoDB could not satisfy from the buffer pool and had to read directly from the disk.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool reads per second
items.description: Number of logical reads per second that InnoDB could not satisfy from the buffer pool and had to read directly from the disk.

items.name: InnoDB row lock time
items.description: The total time spent in acquiring row locks for InnoDB tables, in milliseconds.

items.name: InnoDB row lock time max
items.description: The maximum time to acquire a row lock for InnoDB tables, in milliseconds.

items.name: InnoDB row lock waits
items.description: Number of times operations on InnoDB tables had to wait for a row lock.

items.name: Max used connections
items.description: The maximum number of connections that have been in use simultaneously since the server start.

items.name: Queries per second
items.description: Number of statements executed by the server. This variable includes statements executed within stored programs, unlike the `Questions` variable.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool read requests
items.description: Number of logical read requests.

items.name: Questions per second
items.description: Number of statements executed by the server. This includes only statements sent to the server by clients and not statements executed within stored programs, unlike the `Queries` variable.

items.name: Slow queries per second
items.description: Number of queries that have taken more than `long_query_time` seconds.

items.name: Threads cached
items.description: Number of threads in the thread cache.

items.name: Threads connected
items.description: Number of currently open connections.

items.name: Threads running
items.description: Number of threads that are not sleeping.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool read requests per second
items.description: Number of logical read requests per second.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool pages total
items.description: The total size of the InnoDB buffer pool, in pages.

items.name: Bytes received
items.description: Number of bytes received from all clients.

items.name: Connection errors max connections per second
items.description: Number of refused connections due to the `max_connections` limit being reached.

items.name: Bytes sent
items.description: Number of bytes sent to all clients.

items.name: Command Delete per second
items.description: The `Com_delete` counter variable indicates the number of times the `DELETE` statement has been executed.

items.name: Command Insert per second
items.description: The `Com_insert` counter variable indicates the number of times the `INSERT` statement has been executed.

items.name: Command Select per second
items.description: The `Com_select` counter variable indicates the number of times the `SELECT` statement has been executed.

items.name: Command Update per second
items.description: The `Com_update` counter variable indicates the number of times the `UPDATE` statement has been executed.

items.name: Connection errors accept per second
items.description: Number of errors that occurred during calls to `accept()` on the listening port.

items.name: Connection errors internal per second
items.description: Number of refused connections due to internal server errors, for example, out of memory errors, or failed thread starts.

items.name: Connection errors peer address per second
items.description: Number of errors while searching for the connecting client's IP address.

items.name: InnoDB buffer pool pages free
items.description: The total size of the InnoDB buffer pool, in pages.

items.name: Connection errors select per second
items.description: Number of errors during calls to `select()` or `poll()` on the listening port. The client would not necessarily have been rejected in these cases.

items.name: Connection errors tcpwrap per second
items.description: Number of connections the libwrap library has refused.

items.name: Connections per second
items.description: Number of connection attempts (successful or not) to the MySQL server.

items.name: Aborted connections per second
items.description: Number of failed attempts to connect to the MySQL server.

items.name: Aborted clients per second
items.description: Number of connections that were aborted because the client died without closing the connection properly.

items.name: Threads created per second
items.description: Number of threads created to handle connections. If the value of `Threads_created` is large, you may want to increase the `thread_cache_size` value. The cache miss rate can be calculated as `Threads_created`/`Connections`.

items.name: Created tmp tables on disk per second
items.description: Number of internal on-disk temporary tables created by the server while executing statements.

items.name: Created tmp files on disk per second
items.description: How many temporary files `mysqld` has created.

items.name: Created tmp tables on memory per second
items.description: Number of internal temporary tables created by the server while executing statements.

items.name: Innodb buffer pool wait free
items.description: Number of times InnoDB waited for a free page before reading or creating a page. Normally, writes to the InnoDB buffer pool happen in the background. When no clean pages are available, dirty pages are flushed first in order to free some up. This counts the numbers of wait for this operation to finis

items.name: Innodb number open files
items.description: Number of open files held by InnoDB. InnoDB only.

items.name: Innodb log written
items.description: Number of bytes written to the InnoDB log.

items.name: Open tables
items.description: Number of tables that are open.

items.name: Open table definitions
items.description: Number of cached table definitions.

items.name: Binlog cache disk use
items.description: Number of transactions that used a temporary disk cache because they could not fit in the regular binary log cache, being larger than `binlog_cache_size`.

item_prototypes.name: Size of database {#DATABASE}
item_prototypes.description: Database size.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Slave status {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description: The item gets status information on the essential parameters of the slave threads.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Seconds Behind Master {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description:
Number of seconds that the slave SQL thread is behind processing the master binary log.
A high number (or an increasing one) can indicate that the slave is unable to handle events
from the master in a timely fashion.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Slave IO Running {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description:
Whether the I/O thread for reading the master's binary log is running.
Normally, you want this to be Yes unless you have not yet started a replication or have
explicitly stopped it with STOP SLAVE.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Slave SQL Running {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description:
Whether the SQL thread for executing events in the relay log is running.
As with the I/O thread, this should normally be Yes.

item_prototypes.name: Binlog commits
item_prototypes.description: Total number of transactions committed to the binary log.

item_prototypes.name: Binlog group commits
item_prototypes.description: Total number of group commits done to the binary log.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait count
item_prototypes.description: The number of times `MASTER_GTID_WAIT` called.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait timeouts
item_prototypes.description: Number of timeouts occurring in `MASTER_GTID_WAIT`.

item_prototypes.name: Master GTID wait time
item_prototypes.description: Total number of time spent in `MASTER_GTID_WAIT`.

item_prototypes.name: Replication Slave SQL Running State {#MASTER_HOST}
item_prototypes.description: This shows the state of the SQL driver threads.

lld_rules.description: If "show slave status" returns Master_Host, "Replication: *" items are created.

lld_rules.description: Used for additional metrics if MariaDB is used.

items.name: ICMP loss
items.description: The percentage of lost packets.

items.name: ICMP response time
items.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

items.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: ICMP ping
System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of th

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface,including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and another times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of t

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them fro

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in bits per second.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no accurate estimation can be made,
this object should contain the nominal bandwidth.
If the bandwidth of the interface is greater than the maximum va

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Duplex status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: EtherLike-MIB
The current mode of operation of the MAC
entity.  'unknown' indicates that the current
duplex mode could not be determined.

Management control of the duplex mode is
accomplished through the MAU MIB.  When
an interface does not support autonegotiation,
or when autonegotiat

items.name: Version
items.description: The Oracle Server version.

items.name: Sessions lock rate
items.description: The percentage of locked sessions. Locks are mechanisms that prevent destructive interaction between transactions accessing the same resource - either user objects, such as tables and rows or system objects not visible to users, such as shared data structures in memory and data dictionary rows.

items.name: Physical writes per second
items.description: Writes per second.

items.name: Number of processes
items.description: The current number of user processes.

items.name: Processes limit
items.description: The maximum number of user processes.

items.name: Redo logs available to switch
items.description: The number of inactive/unused redo logs available for log switching.

items.name: Rows per sort
items.description: The average number of rows per sort for all types of sorts performed.

items.name: SQL service response time
items.description: The Structured Query Language (SQL) service response time expressed in seconds.

items.name: Active background sessions
items.description: The number of active background sessions.

items.name: Active user sessions
items.description: The number of active user sessions.

items.name: Sessions concurrency
items.description: The percentage of concurrency. Concurrency is a database behavior when different transactions request to change the same resource. In the case of modifying data transactions, it sequentially temporarily blocks the right to change the data, and the rest of the transactions wait for access. When the a

items.name: Session count
items.description: The session count.

items.name: Inactive user sessions
items.description: The number of inactive user sessions.

items.name: Sessions limit
items.description: The user and system sessions.

items.name: Sessions locked over {$ORACLE.SESSION.LOCK.MAX.TIME}s
items.description: The count of the prolongedly locked sessions. (You can change the duration of the maximum session lock in seconds for a query using the `{$ORACLE.SESSION.LOCK.MAX.TIME}` macro. Default = 600 s).

items.name: User rollbacks per second
items.description: The number of times that users manually issued the `ROLLBACK` statement or an error occurred during the users' transactions.

items.name: SGA, buffer cache
items.description: The size of standard block cache.

items.name: Physical writes bytes per second
items.description: Write bytes per second.

items.name: SGA, java pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from the Java pool.

items.name: SGA, large pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from a large pool.

items.name: SGA, log buffer
items.description: The number of bytes allocated for the redo log buffer.

items.name: SGA, shared pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from a shared pool.

items.name: Shared pool free %
items.description: Free memory of a shared pool expressed in %.

items.name: Total sorts per user call
items.description: The total sorts per user call.

items.name: Temp space used
items.description: Used temporary space.

items.name: PGA, Total allocated
items.description: The current amount of the PGA memory allocated by the instance. The Oracle Database attempts to keep this number below the value of the `PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET` initialization parameter. However, it is possible for the PGA allocated to exceed that value by a small percentage and for a short period of

items.name: PGA, Total freeable
items.description: The number of bytes of the PGA memory in all processes that could be freed back to the OS.

items.name: PGA, Total inuse
items.description: The amount of Program Global Area (PGA) memory currently consumed by work areas. This number can be used to determine how much memory is consumed by other consumers of the PGA memory (for example, PL/SQL or Java).

items.name: Uptime
items.description: The Oracle instance uptime expressed in seconds.

items.name: User '{$ORACLE.USER}' expire password
items.description: The number of days before the Zabbix account password expires.

items.name: SGA, fixed
items.description: The fixed System Global Area (SGA) is an internal housekeeping area.

items.name: Physical reads bytes per second
items.description: Read bytes per second.

items.name: Physical reads per second
items.description: Reads per second.

items.name: Enqueue timeouts per second
items.description: Enqueue timeouts per second.

items.name: Active parallel sessions
items.description: The number of active parallel sessions.

items.name: Active serial sessions
items.description: The number of active serial sessions.

items.name: Average active sessions
items.description: The average number of active sessions at a point in time that are either working or waiting.

items.name: Archiver state
items.description: The status of automatic archiving.

items.name: Buffer cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of buffer cache hits ((LogRead - PhyRead)/LogRead).

items.name: Global cache blocks corrupted
items.description: The number of blocks that encountered corruption or checksum failure during the interconnect.

items.name: Global cache blocks lost
items.description: The number of lost global cache blocks.

items.name: Cursor cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of cursor cache hits (CursorCacheHit/SoftParse).

items.name: Database CPU time ratio
items.description: The ratio calculated by dividing the total CPU (used by the database) by the Oracle time model statistic DB time.

items.name: Database wait time ratio
items.description: Wait time - the time that the server process spends waiting for available shared resources to be released by other server processes such as latches, locks, data buffers, etc.

items.name: Datafiles count
items.description: The current number of datafiles.

items.name: Datafiles limit
items.description: The maximum allowable number of datafiles.

items.name: Disk sort per second
items.description: The number of sorts going to disk per second.

items.name: FRA, Number of files
items.description: The number of files in the FRA.

items.name: PGA, Aggregate target parameter
items.description: The current value of the `PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET` initialization parameter. If this parameter is not set, then its value is "0" and automatic management of the PGA memory is disabled.

items.name: FRA, Number of restore points
items.description: Number of restore points in the FRA.

items.name: FRA, Space limit
items.description: The maximum amount of disk space (in bytes) that the database can use for the Fast Recovery Area (FRA).

items.name: FRA, Space reclaimable
items.description: The total amount of disk space (in bytes) that can be created by deleting obsolete, redundant, and other low-priority files from the FRA.

items.name: FRA, Used space
items.description: The amount of disk space (in bytes) used by FRA files created in the current and all the previous FRAs.

items.name: FRA, Usable space in %
items.description: Percentage of space usable in the FRA.

items.name: GC CR block received per second
items.description: The global cache (GC) and the consistent read (CR) block received per second.

items.name: Instance hostname
items.description: The name of the host machine.

items.name: Instance name
items.description: The name of the instance.

items.name: Instance status
items.description: The status of the instance.

items.name: Instance role
items.description: Indicates whether the instance is an active instance or an inactive secondary instance.

items.name: Library cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of library cache hits (Hits/Pins).

items.name: Logons per second
items.description: The number of logon attempts.

items.name: Long table scans per second
items.description: The number of long table scans per second. A table is considered long if it is not cached and if its high water mark is greater than five blocks.

items.name: PGA, Global memory bound
items.description: The maximum size of a work area executed in automatic mode.

items.name: Memory sorts ratio
items.description: The percentage of sorts (from `ORDER BY` clauses or index building) that are done to disk vs. in-memory.

items.name: Sessions concurrency
items.description: The percentage of concurrency. Concurrency is a database behavior when different transactions request to change the same resource. In the case of modifying data transactions, it sequentially temporarily blocks the right to change the data, and the rest of the transactions wait for access. When the a

items.name: Session count
items.description: The session count.

items.name: Inactive user sessions
items.description: The number of inactive user sessions.

items.name: SQL service response time
items.description: The Structured Query Language (SQL) service response time expressed in seconds.

items.name: Sessions limit
items.description: The user and system sessions.

items.name: Sessions lock rate
items.description: The percentage of locked sessions. Locks are mechanisms that prevent destructive interaction between transactions accessing the same resource - either user objects, such as tables and rows or system objects not visible to users, such as shared data structures in memory and data dictionary rows.

items.name: Processes limit
items.description: The maximum number of user processes.

items.name: Active parallel sessions
items.description: The number of active parallel sessions.

items.name: Sessions locked over {$ORACLE.SESSION.LOCK.MAX.TIME}s
items.description: The count of the prolongedly locked sessions. (You can change the duration of the maximum session lock in seconds for a query using the `{$ORACLE.SESSION.LOCK.MAX.TIME}` macro. Default = 600 s).

items.name: SGA, buffer cache
items.description: The size of standard block cache.

items.description: The fixed System Global Area (SGA) is an internal housekeeping area.

items.name: SGA, java pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from the Java pool.

items.name: SGA, log buffer
items.description: The number of bytes allocated for the redo log buffer.

items.name: SGA, shared pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from a shared pool.

items.name: Shared pool free %
items.description: Free memory of a shared pool expressed in %.

items.name: Total sorts per user call
items.description: The total sorts per user call.

items.name: Temp space used
items.description: Used temporary space.

items.description: The current amount of the PGA memory allocated by the instance. The Oracle Database attempts to keep this number below the value of the `PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET` initialization parameter. However, it is possible for the PGA allocated to exceed that value by a small percentage and for a short period of

items.name: PGA, Total freeable
items.description: The number of bytes of the PGA memory in all processes that could be freed back to the OS.

items.name: PGA, Total inuse
items.description: The amount of Program Global Area (PGA) memory currently consumed by work areas. This number can be used to determine how much memory is consumed by other consumers of the PGA memory (for example, PL/SQL or Java).

items.name: User rollbacks per second
items.description: The number of times that users manually issued the `ROLLBACK` statement or an error occurred during the users' transactions.

items.name: SGA, large pool
items.description: The memory is allocated from a large pool.

items.name: Physical reads bytes per second
items.description: Read bytes per second.

items.name: Physical writes per second
items.description: Writes per second.

items.name: FRA, Space reclaimable
items.description: The total amount of disk space (in bytes) that can be created by deleting obsolete, redundant, and other low-priority files from the FRA.

items.name: Average active sessions
items.description: The average number of active sessions at a point in time that are either working or waiting.

items.name: Archiver state
items.description: The status of automatic archiving.

items.name: Buffer cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of buffer cache hits ((LogRead - PhyRead)/LogRead).

items.name: Global cache blocks corrupted
items.description: The number of blocks that encountered corruption or checksum failure during the interconnect.

items.name: Global cache blocks lost
items.description: The number of lost global cache blocks.

items.name: Cursor cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of cursor cache hits (CursorCacheHit/SoftParse).

items.name: Database CPU time ratio
items.description: The ratio calculated by dividing the total CPU (used by the database) by the Oracle time model statistic DB time.

items.name: Database wait time ratio
items.description: Wait time - the time that the server process spends waiting for available shared resources to be released by other server processes such as latches, locks, data buffers, etc.

items.name: Datafiles limit
items.description: The maximum allowable number of datafiles.

items.name: Disk sort per second
items.description: The number of sorts going to disk per second.

items.name: Enqueue timeouts per second
items.description: Enqueue timeouts per second.

items.name: FRA, Number of files
items.description: The number of files in the FRA.

items.name: FRA, Number of restore points
items.description: Number of restore points in the FRA.

items.name: FRA, Space limit
items.description: The maximum amount of disk space (in bytes) that the database can use for the Fast Recovery Area (FRA).

items.name: FRA, Used space
items.description: The amount of disk space (in bytes) used by FRA files created in the current and all the previous FRAs.

items.name: Physical writes bytes per second
items.description: Write bytes per second.

items.name: FRA, Usable space in %
items.description: Percentage of space usable in the FRA.

items.name: GC CR block received per second
items.description: The global cache (GC) and the consistent read (CR) block received per second.

items.name: Instance status
items.description: The status of the instance.

items.name: Instance role
items.description: Indicates whether the instance is an active instance or an inactive secondary instance.

items.name: Library cache hit ratio
items.description: The ratio of library cache hits (Hits/Pins).

items.name: Logons per second
items.description: The number of logon attempts.

items.name: Long table scans per second
items.description: The number of long table scans per second. A table is considered long if it is not cached and if its high water mark is greater than five blocks.

items.name: Memory sorts ratio
items.description: The percentage of sorts (from `ORDER BY` clauses or index building) that are done to disk vs. in-memory.

items.name: PGA, Global memory bound
items.description: The maximum size of a work area executed in automatic mode.

items.name: PGA, Aggregate target parameter
items.description: The current value of the `PGA_AGGREGATE_TARGET` initialization parameter. If this parameter is not set, then its value is "0" and automatic management of the PGA memory is disabled.

items.name: Active serial sessions
items.description: The number of active serial sessions.

items.name: Physical reads per second
items.description: Reads per second.

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description: It checks whether the RabbitMQ application is running; and whether the channels and queues can be listed successfully; and that no alarms are in effect.

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck: expiration date on the certificates{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description:
It checks the expiration date on the certificates for every listener configured to use the Transport Layer Security (TLS).
It responds with a status code `200 OK` if all the certificates are valid (have not expired).
Otherwise, it responds with a status code `503 Service Unavailable`.

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck: local alarms in effect on this node{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description:
It responds with a status code `200 OK` if there are no alarms in effect in the cluster.
Otherwise, it responds with a status code `503 Service Unavailable`.

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck: classic mirrored queues without synchronized mirrors online{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description:
It checks if there are classic mirrored queues without synchronized mirrors online (queues that would potentially lose data if the target node is shut down).
It responds with a status code `200 OK` if there are no such classic mirrored queues.
Otherwise, it responds with a status code `503 Service

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck: queues with minimum online quorum{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description:
It checks if there are quorum queues with minimum online quorum (queues that would lose their quorum and availability if the target node is shut down).
It responds with a status code `200 OK` if there are no such quorum queues.
Otherwise, it responds with a status code `503 Service Unavailable`.

item_prototypes.name: Healthcheck: virtual hosts on this node{#SINGLETON}
item_prototypes.description:
It responds with It responds with a status code `200 OK` if all virtual hosts are running on the target node.
Otherwise it responds with a status code `503 Service Unavailable`.

item_prototypes.name: Deployment [{#DEPLOYMENT}]: Enabled time
item_prototypes.description: Indicates if the deployment is managed (aka uses the ContentRepository).

item_prototypes.name: Deployment [{#DEPLOYMENT}]: Enabled
item_prototypes.description: Boolean indicating whether the deployment content is currently deployed in the runtime (or should be deployed in the runtime the next time the server starts).

item_prototypes.name: Deployment [{#DEPLOYMENT}]: Managed
item_prototypes.description: Indicates if the deployment is managed (aka uses the ContentRepository).

item_prototypes.name: Deployment [{#DEPLOYMENT}]: Persistent
item_prototypes.description: Indicates if the deployment is managed (aka uses the ContentRepository).

item_prototypes.name: Deployment [{#DEPLOYMENT}]: Status
item_prototypes.description:
The current runtime status of a deployment.
Possible status modes are OK, FAILED, and STOPPED.
FAILED indicates a dependency is missing or a service could not start.
STOPPED indicates that the deployment was not enabled or was manually stopped.

items.name: ICMP ping
ICMP loss
items.description: The percentage of lost packets.

items.name: ICMP response time
items.description: The ICMP ping response time (in seconds).

items.name: SNMP traps (fallback)
items.description: The item is used to collect all SNMP traps unmatched by other snmptrap items

items.name: System contact details
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The textual identification of the contact person for this managed node, together with information on how to contact this person. If no contact information is known, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: CPU utilization
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The average, over the last minute, of the percentage of time that processors was not idle.
Implementations may approximate this one minute smoothing period if necessary.

items.name: System description
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

items.name: System location
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

items.name: System name
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

items.name: System object ID
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example

items.name: Uptime (network)
items.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Time (in hundredths of a second) since the network management portion of the system was last re-initialized.

items.name: SNMP agent availability
items.description:
Availability of SNMP checks on the host. The value of this item corresponds to availability icons in the host list.
Possible values:
0 - not available
1 - available
2 - unknown

items.name: Uptime (hardware)
items.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of time since this host was last initialized. Note that this is different from sysUpTime in the SNMPv2-MIB [RFC1907] because sysUpTime is the uptime of the network management portion of the system.

items.name: Windows: SNMP walk network interfaces
items.description: Discovering interfaces from IF-MIB.

items.name: Windows: SNMP walk mounted filesystems
items.description: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB::hrStorage discovery.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of inbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of th

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Inbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of inbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of inbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them from

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits received
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets received on the interface,including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and another times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets discarded
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The number of outbound packets which were chosen to be discarded
even though no errors had been detected to prevent their being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.
One possible reason for discarding such a packet could be to free up buffer space.
Discontinuities in the value of t

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Outbound packets with errors
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
For packet-oriented interfaces, the number of outbound packets that contained errors preventing them from being deliverable to a higher-layer protocol.  For character-oriented or fixed-length interfaces, the number of outbound transmission units that contained errors preventing them fro

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Bits sent
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The total number of octets transmitted out of the interface, including framing characters. Discontinuities in the value of this counter can occur at re-initialization of the management system, and at other times as indicated by the value of ifCounterDiscontinuityTime.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
An estimate of the interface's current bandwidth in units of 1,000,000 bits per second. If this object reports a value of `n`, then the speed of the interface is somewhere in the range of `n-500,000` to `n+499,999`.
For interfaces which do not vary in bandwidth or for those where no ac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The current operational state of the interface.
- The testing(3) state indicates that no operational packet scan be passed
- If ifAdminStatus is down(2) then ifOperStatus should be down(2)
- If ifAdminStatus is changed to up(1) then ifOperStatus should change to up(1) if the interfac

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: IF-MIB
The type of interface.
Additional values for ifType are assigned by the Internet Assigned Numbers Authority (IANA),
through updating the syntax of the IANAifType textual convention.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Get data
item_prototypes.description:
HOST-RESOURCES-MIB::hrStorage.
Intermediate data for subsequent processing.

item_prototypes.name: {#MEMNAME}: Get data
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used, in %
item_prototypes.description: The space utilization expressed in % for {#FSNAME}.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Total
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.
This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system.
For e

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSNAME}]: Space: Used
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

item_prototypes.name: {#MEMNAME}: Total
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The size of the storage represented by this entry, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.
This object is writable to allow remote configuration of the size of the storage area in those cases where such an operation makes sense and is possible on the underlying system.
For e

item_prototypes.name: {#MEMNAME}: Used
item_prototypes.description:
MIB: HOST-RESOURCES-MIB
The amount of the storage represented by this entry that is allocated, in units of hrStorageAllocationUnits.

item_prototypes.name: {#MEMNAME}: Utilization
item_prototypes.description: Memory utilization in %.

items.description: The Processor Queue Length shows the number of threads that are observed as delayed in the processor Ready Queue and are waiting to be executed.

items.name: Context switches per second
items.description:
Context Switches/sec is the combined rate at which all processors on the computer are switched from one thread to another.
Context switches occur when a running thread voluntarily relinquishes the processor, is preempted by a higher priority ready thread, or switches between user-mode and privilege

items.name: CPU user time
items.description: The processor information `% User Time` counter shows the percent of time that the processor(s) is spent executing in User mode.

items.name: CPU privileged time
items.description: The processor information `% Privileged Time` counter shows the percent of time that the processor is spent executing in Kernel (or Privileged) mode. Privileged mode includes services interrupts inside Interrupt Service Routines (ISRs), executing Deferred Procedure Calls (DPCs), Device Driver calls

items.name: CPU interrupt time
items.description: The processor information `% Interrupt Time` counter indicates how much time the processor spends handling hardware interrupts during sample intervals. It reflects the activity of devices like the system clock, mouse, disk drivers, and network cards. A value above 20% suggests possible hardware issu

items.name: CPU DPC time
items.description: Processor DPC time is the time that a single processor spent receiving and servicing deferred procedure calls (DPCs). DPCs are interrupts that run at a lower priority than standard interrupts. `% DPC Time` is a component of `% Privileged Time` because DPCs are executed in privileged mode. If a high

items.name: Used swap space in %
items.description: The used space of swap volume/file in percent.

items.name: Memory pool non-paged
items.description:
This measures the size, in bytes, of the non-paged pool. This is an area of system memory for objects that cannot be written to disk but instead must remain in physical memory as long as they are allocated.
There is a possible memory leak if the value is greater than 175MB (or 100MB with the /3GB s

items.name: Memory pages per second
items.description:
This measures the rate at which pages are read from or written to disk to resolve hard page faults.
If the value is greater than 1,000, as a result of excessive paging, there may be a memory leak.

items.name: Memory page faults per second
items.description: Page Faults/sec is the average number of pages faulted per second. It is measured in number of pages faulted per second because only one page is faulted in each fault operation, hence this is also equal to the number of page fault operations. This counter includes both hard faults (those that requir

items.name: Free system page table entries
items.description: This indicates the number of page table entries not currently in use by the system. If the number is less than 5,000, there may be a memory leak or you running out of memory.

items.name: Cache bytes
items.description: Cache Bytes is the sum of the Memory\\System Cache Resident Bytes, Memory\\System Driver Resident Bytes, Memory\\System Code Resident Bytes, and Memory\\Pool Paged Resident Bytes counters. This counter displays the last observed value only; it is not an average.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk utilization by idle time
item_prototypes.description: This item is the percentage of elapsed time that the selected disk drive was busy servicing read or writes requests based on idle time.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Average disk read queue length
item_prototypes.description: Average disk read queue, the number of requests outstanding on the disk at the time the performance data is collected.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read request avg waiting time
item_prototypes.description: The average time for read requests issued to the device to be served. This includes the time spent by the requests in queue and the time spent servicing them.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write request avg waiting time
item_prototypes.description: The average time for write requests issued to the device to be served. This includes the time spent by the requests in queue and the time spent servicing them.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Average disk write queue length
item_prototypes.description: Average disk write queue, the number of requests outstanding on the disk at the time the performance data is collected.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk average queue size (avgqu-sz)
item_prototypes.description: The current average disk queue; the number of requests outstanding on the disk while the performance data is being collected.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk read rate
item_prototypes.description: Rate of read operations on the disk.

item_prototypes.name: {#DEVNAME}: Disk write rate
item_prototypes.description: Rate of write operations on the disk.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Speed
item_prototypes.description: Estimated bandwidth of the network interface if any.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Operational status
item_prototypes.description: The operational status of the network interface.

item_prototypes.name: Interface {#IFNAME}({#IFALIAS}): Interface type
item_prototypes.description: The type of the network interface.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSLABEL}({#FSNAME})]: Get data
item_prototypes.description: Intermediate data of `{#FSNAME}` filesystem.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSLABEL}({#FSNAME})]: Space: Used, in %
item_prototypes.description: Calculated as the percentage of currently used space compared to the maximum available space.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSLABEL}({#FSNAME})]: Space: Total
item_prototypes.description: Total space expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSLABEL}({#FSNAME})]: Space: Used
item_prototypes.description: Used storage expressed in bytes.

item_prototypes.name: FS [{#FSLABEL}({#FSNAME})]: Space: Available
item_prototypes.description: Available storage space expressed in bytes.

A textual description of the entity. This value should
include the full name and version identification of the system's hardware type, software operating-system, and
networking software.

system.location[sysLocation.0].description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
Physical location of the node (e.g., `equipment room`, `3rd floor`). If not provided, the value is a zero-length string.

system.name.name: System name
system.name.description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
An administratively-assigned name for this managed node.By convention, this is the node's fully-qualified domain name. If the name is unknown, the value is the zero-length string.

system.objectid[sysObjectID.0].name: System object ID
system.objectid[sysObjectID.0].description:
MIB: SNMPv2-MIB
The vendor's authoritative identification of the network management subsystem contained in the entity. This value is allocated within the SMI enterprises subtree (1.3.6.1.4.1) and provides an easy and unambiguous means for determining`what kind of box' is being managed.  For example
